actions.name: ma000001-0000-0000-0000-000000000001
actions.displayName: Stage 1 — Check Library for Known Game

input.prompt:
You are the Misconception Architect's Library Router. A game concept has just received a GO decision. Your job is to check whether this game concept overlaps with any known game in the Misconception Library, and if so, extract all applicable seeded misconception entries.

Known games with seeded entries:
- Bakery Rush: counting-up, carry/regrouping errors, visual-shape matching
- Fire Dispatch: repeated-addition, division-as-subtraction
- Unit Circle Pizza Lab: radian/degree confusion, sin/cos axis flip
- Power Grid Operator: equals-as-command, inverse-operation confusion
- School Trip Fleet: ceiling division truncation, per-vehicle vs total capacity confusion

For the incoming concept:
1. Identify if it belongs to the same interaction family as any known game
2. List which seeded misconception entries are DIRECTLY RELEVANT (will transfer unchanged)
3. List which seeded entries need REVISION because the brief changes the confusion risk
4. Identify what NEW misconception risks this concept introduces that have no library entry

Be specific. Name the exact seeded entry. Explain why it transfers or why it needs revision.

library_coverage.description: How well does the existing library cover this concept's misconception space?

risk.description: Plain-language description of the misconception risk

library_action.description: KEEP = seeded entry transfers unchanged. REVISE = needs rewrite. NEW = no library entry exists.

misconception_risks.description: All identified misconception risks for this concept

categories_needing_new_entries.description: Error categories that need brand new library entries generated

input.prompt:
You are the Misconception Architect. Generate a complete misconception map for this game concept. For each risk identified, produce a structured library entry following this exact format:

---
MISCONCEPTION: [plain-language name]
CATEGORY: [procedural_error | representation_mismatch | conceptual_gap | overgeneralization | shortcut_skill | cognitive_overload]
DESCRIPTION: [What exactly does the player do wrong? Be specific about the wrong operation or reasoning pattern.]
FAILURE SIGNAL: [What observable behavior in the game reveals this misconception? What would the designer or researcher see?]
DESIGN RESPONSE: [What specific design decision, mechanic, feedback, or constraint addresses this misconception?]
APPLIES TO FUTURE GAMES WHEN: [What conditions in a future game would make this entry relevant again?]
LIBRARY ACTION: [KEEP | REVISE | NEW]
SEVERITY: [HIGH | MEDIUM | LOW]
---

Do not produce generic entries. Every entry must be grounded in the specific mechanics, age band, and skill of this game.

actions.name: ma000004-0000-0000-0000-000000000004
actions.displayName: Stage 4 — Write Misconception Map to Library (Pending)

actions.name: ma000005-0000-0000-0000-000000000005
actions.displayName: Stage 5 — Add Concept to Prototype Specs

input.prompt:
You are the Pipeline Orchestrator. A concept has just completed the Misconception Architect stage. Write a brief status update (3-5 sentences) confirming:
1. The concept name and that it passed GO
2. How many misconception entries were generated (KEEP / REVISE / NEW breakdown)
3. What the highest-severity risk is and the design response
4. The recommended next action for the team (which prototype phase to enter and what to prove first)
